question_scores.text: How does your organization manage build artifacts (binaries, containers, packages)?
How are container images or deployment packages versioned?
How does your team manage third-party dependencies?
Is there a software supply chain security standard in place (e.g., SLSA, SSDF)?
How are builds triggered in your primary CI/CD pipeline?
What is your team's primary branching strategy?
How long does your average build take from commit to test results?
Do you use any of the following build optimization techniques?
How does your organization manage pipeline configurations across services?
How are deployments to production triggered?
How does your team manage environment promotion (dev → staging → prod)?
What is your rollback strategy when a production deployment fails?
How frequently does your team deploy to production?
Does your team use feature flags to decouple deployment from release?
How quickly do developers get feedback on a code change (from commit to test results)?
How long does it take a new developer to make their first production-ready commit?
Does your organization have an internal developer portal or service catalog?
How does your organization measure developer experience and satisfaction?
How would you describe the engineering culture around CI/CD and automation?
What percentage of your infrastructure is managed as code?
How are infrastructure changes applied?
How are development environments provisioned?
Does your organization maintain a module/template catalog for IaC?
What monitoring is in place for your applications and infrastructure?
Are SLOs (Service Level Objectives) defined and tracked?
Can you correlate deployment events with changes in application behavior?
What is your mean time to detect (MTTD) production incidents?
How are pipeline and CI/CD system failures monitored?
How are CI/CD pipeline configurations managed across your organization?
How are changes to shared pipeline templates rolled out?
Can your organization respond to a critical pipeline vulnerability (e.g., compromised action/plugin) across all services?
How is pipeline execution audited and governed for compliance?
How are secrets and credentials managed in your CI/CD pipelines?
Which security scanning stages are integrated into your CI/CD pipeline?
Does your organization enforce policy-as-code for compliance?
How is your Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) managed?
How quickly can your team respond to a critical zero-day vulnerability (e.g., Log4Shell)?
Is there an audit trail for all pipeline changes and deployments?
What is the approximate automated test coverage across your codebase?
How does your team implement the test pyramid?
How are flaky tests managed in your CI pipeline?
What code quality gates are enforced before merging to main?
Does your team practice shift-left security testing?
How are data quality checks handled in your data pipelines (if applicable)?
How are Databricks jobs and pipelines defined and deployed?
How does your team authenticate Databricks CI/CD pipelines?
How are Databricks cluster configurations managed for production workloads?
What types of automated tests exist for your data pipelines?
How are data contracts between pipeline stages or teams managed?
How is data quality monitored in production?
How are Delta Live Tables (DLT) pipelines created and managed?
What is the coverage of DLT expectations (data quality rules) in your pipelines?
How are DLT pipeline configurations parameterized across environments?
Where does your team primarily develop Databricks code?
How are production workloads executed?
How is shared logic (utilities, transformations) reused across Databricks projects?
What is your organization's Unity Catalog adoption status?
How are Unity Catalog permissions and grants managed?
How is data lineage tracked and used in your Databricks environment?

phases.name: 30-Day Quick Wins
phases.description: High-impact, low-effort improvements that deliver immediate value

items.title: Implement Dependency Management
items.description: Set up automated dependency updates with vulnerability gating. Use a private registry mirror for supply chain security.

items.title: Adopt Feature Flags
items.description: Implement feature flag management to decouple deployment from release. Enable gradual rollouts and instant kill switches.

items.title: Integrate Security Scanning in CI
items.description: Add SAST, SCA (dependency scanning), and secrets detection to every PR build. Block merges on critical findings.

items.title: Implement Flaky Test Detection & Quarantine
items.description: Deploy automated flaky test detection (fail-then-pass on same commit). Quarantine identified flaky tests so they report but don't block. Track 'PRs Impacted' metric.

items.title: Enforce Code Quality Gates
items.description: Require linting, unit tests, coverage thresholds, and peer review before merge. Configure as required status checks in branch protection.

items.title: Implement Build Caching & Parallelization
items.description: Add dependency caching, parallel test execution, and incremental builds to reduce feedback loop to under 10 minutes.

items.title: Implement Automated Rollback
items.description: Configure health check-based automated rollback for production deployments. Add canary or blue-green deployment strategy.

items.title: Implement GitOps for Infrastructure
items.description: Adopt GitOps reconciliation pattern where infrastructure state is continuously synced from Git. Add drift detection and automatic remediation.

items.title: Define and Track SLOs
items.description: Define Service Level Objectives for critical services with error budgets. Implement burn rate alerting and SLO-based deployment gates.

phases.name: 90-Day Strategic Investments
phases.description: High-impact improvements requiring significant effort but essential for maturity advancement

items.title: Implement Developer Portal
items.description: Deploy an internal developer portal with service catalog, API docs, golden path templates, and service scorecards.

items.title: Implement Golden Pipeline with Managed Inheritance
items.description: Implement a master pipeline definition that services inherit by reference. Add Insert Blocks for team-specific customization while maintaining mandatory governance stages.

items.title: Implement Policy-as-Code
items.description: Define and enforce organizational policies programmatically. Gate deployments on policy compliance checks.

phases.name: 6-Month Maturity Advancement
phases.description: Lower-impact but easy wins that round out the maturity profile

phases.name: 12-Month Comprehensive Plan
phases.description: Long-term improvements to consider after higher-priority items are complete
